subject: Statistics
title: The McNamara Fallacy
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The McNamara Fallacy.

hook.text: In Vietnam, the US measured success by body count. They were 'winning' by the numbers and losing the war.

buildup.text: Robert McNamara quantified everything measurable and ignored everything that wasn't. A fatal error.

discovery.text: The McNamara Fallacy: measuring only what's easy to count and assuming it captures the full picture.

twist.text: Body counts said the US was winning. Morale, political will, and guerrilla effectiveness said otherwise.

climax.text: The fallacy persists in business: measuring engagement clicks while ignoring customer satisfaction.

punchline.text: Not everything that counts can be counted. Not everything counted counts.

quiz.question: What is the McNamara Fallacy?
options: Measuring only what's easy to quantify and ignoring important factors that are hard to measure
Using old data to make modern decisions
